proposals.title: Volatility-scaled triple-barrier labels + strategy-conditional candidate screening (unlock the 3 dead strategies)
proposals.description: Two coupled label-generation fixes in the backtest engine. (a) Replace the fixed-percent barriers in _compute_triple_barrier_label with volatility-scaled barriers per AFML Ch. 3.2: tp/sl = k_tp/k_sl * daily_volatility * sqrt(horizon), exposing k_tp/k_sl in the optimizer param space. (b) Make candidate screening strategy-conditional: screen_at_date already accepts scoring_weights, so give each STRATEGY_REGISTRY entry its own screening profile — mean_reversion gets inverted/oversold-tilted weights (negative momentum_weight, RSI-low preference) so its label function actually sees oversold names; quality_momentum requires quality data present. Validate via existing walk-forward + promotion gates before any live param change.
proposals.roi_rationale: This attacks the project's documented dead-end: 'alt strategies train fine but labels ~all-neutral so only triple_barrier/meta_label ever trade' (rotation machinery 48.1-48.4 built then STOPPED). The mechanism is visible in code: candidate_selector.py:175-206 ranks EVERY strategy's candidate pool by a momentum-weighted composite sorted descending, and backtest_engine.py:395-404/464-466 pass the same weights regardless of self.strategy. Mean-reversion's +1 label (backtest_engine.py:1079-1082) requires sma_50_distance < -5% AND RSI < 35 — a momentum-descending top-50 essentially never contains such names; quality_momentum's -1 leg (lines 1045-1049) requires momentum_6m < -5% — impossible in a momentum-ranked top-50. Labels collapse to {0}, the GradientBoostingClassifier predicts 0 everywhere, 0 trades. Separately, historical_data.py:98-100 computes daily_volatility with the literal comment 'for volatility-adjusted barriers — AFML Ch. 3' yet backtest_engine.py:668-670 uses fixed tp_pct/sl_pct for the whole cross-section — low-vol names ~always time-expire to label 0 while high-vol names dominate the +/-1 classes, degrading the LIVE incumbent's label quality too. Fixing both unlocks the anti-correlated mean-reversion/trend sleeves (strategy_registry.py:81-87 documents the diversification thesis) and improves incumbent OOS robustness at $0 data cost.

proposals.title: EU/KR-native signal parity: market-scoped regime multiplier + KRX short-sale and DART insider overlays
proposals.description: Give the 80 live non-US names (DAX40 + KOSPI seed) their own conviction signals instead of none. Phase 1 (cheapest, also fixes a live mis-scoping): make apply_regime_to_score market-aware — gate the US-FRED conviction multiplier by market_for_symbol(ticker) and add per-market regime series through the existing FRED client (free international series: German 10Y/CPI, Korea CPI) plus yfinance vol indices. Phase 2: KR daily short-selling balance from KRX open data (free) as the short_interest exclusion analog, and DART open API (free key, Korea's EDGAR) for insider/major-shareholder filings as the insider_signal_screen analog. All behind default-OFF settings flags per the established overlay pattern.
proposals.roi_rationale: EU+KR have been live since 2026-06-01, but every conviction overlay in autonomous_loop.py:272-680 is US-sourced: macro_regime (FRED US series, macro_regime.py:41-44), pead_signal (SEC EDGAR 8-K), insider_signal_screen (EDGAR Form 4), short_interest (FINRA CSV, short_interest.py:37), sector_momentum (US SPDR ETFs), analyst_narrative (8-K Exhibit 99), social_velocity (Alpha Vantage US news). Grep for DART/KRX/Naver across backend returns only ticker-suffix logic in markets.py — zero Korean or European data sources exist. So EU/KR candidates compete in the same ranking with no boosts while US names collect them. Worse, apply_regime_to_score (macro_regime.py:526-550, line 542) multiplies EVERY candidate's score by the US-macro conviction multiplier unconditionally — and macro_regime is live (it was one of the 4 SecretStr-dead overlays revived in phase-51.1 per harness_log), meaning KOSPI names are being up/down-weighted today by US CPI/UNRATE/FEDFUNDS. Scoping the regime correctly and adding even two native overlays directly improves signal quality on ~44% of the tradeable name count at zero data cost.

proposals.title: Cross-sectional per-date z-score/rank feature transforms for the ML model
proposals.description: At training-sample build time, compute per-sample-date cross-sectional z-scores (or fractional ranks) of the top-MDA features — momentum_1m/3m/6m/12_1, rsi_14, sma distances, pe_ratio, pb_ratio, quality_score, volume_ratio_20d — against that date's screened candidate cross-section, and append them as parallel feature columns (keeping the raw values). Mirror the same transform at prediction time in _predict_and_trade using the test-date candidate set. Gate via the existing ablation-study machinery and MDA drift logging before promotion.
proposals.roi_rationale: The prediction task is inherently cross-sectional — _predict_and_trade picks the best names among screened candidates at test_start — yet the model never sees cross-sectional information. _build_training_data (backtest_engine.py:551-645) pools raw absolute feature values across dates and tickers; the only transforms are fractional differentiation on 5 non-stationary columns (lines 628-634) and median fill. Grep for zscore/rank across historical_data.py and backtest_engine.py confirms no cross-sectional normalization exists anywhere in the ML path, so the GBM must learn absolute thresholds (e.g. pe_ratio ~ 25, momentum_6m ~ 12%) whose cross-sectional meaning drifts across regimes — a canonical source of regime fragility in pooled cross-sectional classifiers. The live screener already validates the pattern: _apply_multidim_momentum (screener.py:520-580) z-scores its 4 components across the universe. This is the cheapest lift in the set: additive columns, $0 data cost, fully testable through existing walk-forward + ablation surfaces before touching anything live.

proposals.title: Wire the orphaned alt-data tables into the feature vector: FINRA daily short-volume ratio + congress net-buy (IC-gated)
proposals.description: Finish the abandoned last mile of phase-7: (1) re-run the idempotent ingesters (congress.py, finra_short.py) to refresh alt_congress_trades and alt_finra_short_volume; (2) run the existing IC harness (alt_data/features.py) for real — including the never-attempted FINRA leg — and require IC_IR > 0.5 per its own documented threshold; (3) for survivors, add BQ-preloaded feature columns (short_vol_ratio_5d = short_volume/total_volume smoothed, congress_net_usd_20d) to build_feature_vector via the cache bulk-preload pattern, making the candidate_space.yaml bundle 'mda_plus_alt_data_features' real; (4) ablation study through the existing run_ablation_study surface before optimizer adoption.
proposals.roi_rationale: Ingested, compliance-cleared data with zero consumers is pure sunk cost. Evidence: grep for alt_finra_short_volume/alt_congress_trades/alt_13f across backend shows NO consumer outside backend/alt_data/ itself — the tables feed nothing. The IC evaluation that was supposed to validate them never ran: the only results file (backend/backtest/experiments/results/alt_data_ic_20260419T224855.tsv) is header-only; features.py:21 records FINRA as 'NOT attempted (adv_73 owner gate not cleared)' and the 13F row hardcodes ic=0.0 with 'outside this cycle's scope' (features.py:431-447). Meanwhile candidate_space.yaml advertises an 'mda_plus_alt_data_features' bundle that cannot exist — historical_data.py's ~39-column feature vector has no alt-data columns. The daily short-sale volume ratio (finra_short.py columns short_volume/total_volume, lines 48-50) is a distinct, faster signal than the bimonthly shortPercentOfFloat already used by services/short_interest.py, with documented cross-sectional predictive power (Boehmer-Jones-Zhang lineage cited in that module). The IC harness gives a cheap objective go/no-go gate before any model change, so downside is bounded at ~1 day of validation work.

proposals.title: Regime-conditioning via the orphaned VIX regime detector: regime feature column + per-regime rotation champions
proposals.description: Promote the fully-built-but-unused VIXRollingQuantileRegimeDetector from validation-only into signal conditioning, in three additive steps: (a) add vol_regime (low/medium/high categorical from the detector's daily labels) as a feature column so the GBM can learn regime-interaction splits; (b) regime-match sample weighting — multiply the existing average-uniqueness weights by a modest boost for training samples whose regime matches the current one; (c) extend the rotation bake-off (strategy_backtest_adapter) to report per-regime DSR so strategy_selector can maintain per-regime champions instead of one all-weather pick. No hysteresis mechanisms (operator-banned); selection stays gate-driven.
proposals.roi_rationale: The regime detector exists, is tested, and conditions nothing: grep shows regime_detector.py's only consumers are spot_checks.py/spot_checks_harness.py (result validation), settings, and tests — no signal, sizing, label, or selection path reads it, and it defaults OFF (regime_detection_enabled=False). Meanwhile the codebase itself documents the alpha thesis it would serve: strategy_registry.py:81-87 states mean-reversion 'needs choppy/range-bound regimes while the baseline trend/ML needs sustained moves' — yet strategy_selector picks one champion on trailing DSR, blind to the current regime, which is part of why the rotation layer was assessed low-value. The feature vector has zero regime-state columns (historical_data.py's macro features are cpi_yoy/fed_funds/unemployment — no vol-regime state), so the model cannot condition momentum-vs-reversal behavior on volatility state, a well-documented driver of momentum crashes. Uses only VIX data already fetched via yfinance ($0), and complements proposal 1: conditional labels plus regime-aware selection is what makes the rotation machinery actually rotate to the best-earning strategy per the north star.

vetted.title: Volatility-scaled triple-barrier labels + strategy-conditional candidate screening (unlock the 3 dead strategies)
vetted.notes: Half (a) was ALREADY BUILT AND REVERTED: commit 022330ad (2026-03-25, 'Phase 1.2: Volatility-adjusted TB barriers', barriers = daily_vol x multiplier per AFML Ch.3, optimizer-wired) then commit 9fbd9cd6 (2026-03-28) reverted the Phase 1.2-1.9 bundle after a full 27-window walk-forward validated pre-bundle Sharpe 1.0142 and 'confirms the Phase 1.2-1.9 improvements broke the strategy'. The machinery survives as documented dead keys: quant_optimizer.py:88-90 still exposes vol_barrier_multiplier and _apply_params_to_engine:552-555 writes it into engine._strategy_params, but backtest_engine.py never reads it (only screening weights, lines 398-400) -- rotation_runner.py:22-31 explicitly documents this ('DEAD-KEY HONESTY... nothing reads them today. Re-enabling... is its own cycle'). historical_data.py:98-100 daily_volatility computed-and-unused: confirmed. Half (b) strategy-conditional screening is genuinely novel and the mechanism is verified (same weights regardless of self.strategy at backtest_engine.py:397-404/464-466; MR +1 needs sma_dist<-0.05 AND rsi<35 at :1079, QM -1 needs momentum_6m<-5 at :1047; candidate_selector._rank_candidates :174-206 momentum-descending; weight bounds [0,1] can only neutralize, not invert, the momentum tilt; dead-strategy fact pinned by diagnostic commit 6ce1a9c1 2026-05-29). BUT the payoff routes through the strategy-rotation layer the operator explicitly STOPPED as low money value (no deployment bridge -- rotation_runner docstring 'DEFERRED'), or through incumbent label quality where the prior empirical test was NEGATIVE. Validation requires optimizer/walk-forward runs currently barred by the phase-66 durable rule (historical_macro frozen ~103d stale, no optimizer runs) -- needs operator un-freeze first. Not banned, but 3-dev-day estimate ignores re-validation burden, and it modifies the label function behind the live-validated +20%-NAV incumbent (regression risk real, though promotion gates protect live).

vetted.title: EU/KR-native signal parity: market-scoped regime multiplier + KRX short-sale and DART insider overlays
vetted.notes: Gap verified real: grep confirms ZERO Korean/EU data sources (DART/KRX/Naver hits are only trading-calendar/exchange-label/docstring text); _REGIME_SERIES is all-US FRED (macro_regime.py:41-44); apply_regime_to_score has no market parameter and is applied to every screened stock (screener.py:318-322, macro_regime.py:542); the full overlay chain (PEAD/insider/short-interest/sector-momentum/options-flow/narrative/GPR/defense/peer-leadlag/M&A, screener.py:324-412) is US-sourced end to end; econ_calendar has no ECB/BOK sources; none of the done 50.x/51.x steps cover this. Overlay flags follow the default-OFF pattern (settings.py:384ff). CORRECTIONS to the pitch: (1) the uniform conviction multiplier is ranking-NEUTRAL (every candidate multiplied equally) -- the real cross-market skew is only the +/-5% sector tilt plus US-only overlay boosts, so today's 'mis-scoping' is milder than claimed; (2) intl is 80 of ~580 names (~14%), not ~44% (universe_lists.py: EU 40 + KR 40). Money path is DIRECT (live screener drives buys; 066570.KS was bought 06-09), which is rare in this set. Constraints: KRX/DART/FRED-intl are free (OK), but if per-market regimes add Claude regime calls that is metered spend needing operator approval, and the operator's KR appetite is cautious (phase-60.3 KRW-integrity flag still awaiting operator promotion; the away week's KRW quadrillion bug lived exactly in this intl-data surface). 5-dev-day estimate is optimistic for DART corp_code mapping + KRX parsing; phase 1 alone (market-scoping the multiplier + per-market series) is the defensible 1-2 day slice. Enlarges the live attack surface of a working engine -- the operator's stated top risk.

vetted.title: Cross-sectional per-date z-score/rank feature transforms for the ML model
vetted.notes: Claimed gap fully verified: grep for zscore/z_score/.rank( across backtest_engine.py + historical_data.py returns NOTHING; _build_training_data (backtest_engine.py:551-645) pools raw absolute values with only frac-diff on 5 columns + median fill; _NUMERIC_FEATURES (37 cols) has no relative/rank features; meanwhile the live screener already validates the pattern (_zscore + _apply_multidim_momentum, screener.py:487-565, and a default-OFF within-sector percentile mode at :450-475), so the technique has in-repo precedent. Cheapest and least risky of the set: additive parallel columns, $0 data, gated by the existing ablation surface (engine._ablation_mask :227-233, run_ablation_study MCP) and promotion gates. Discounts: money impact is INDIRECT (backtest engine does not trade live money; value flows via optimizer params behind immutable gates plus the MDA cache feeding the live quant_model tool), and the validation runs it needs are currently barred by the phase-66 historical_macro freeze (no optimizer runs) -- so realized P&L impact waits on the operator un-freezing macro ingestion. Best effort-to-soundness ratio here; 1.5-day estimate is credible.

vetted.title: Wire the orphaned alt-data tables into the feature vector: FINRA daily short-volume ratio + congress net-buy (IC-gated)
vetted.notes: Every factual claim verified: alt_congress_trades + alt_finra_short_volume tables EXIST in BQ (DDL confirmed via MCP, schemas match ingesters); grep shows ZERO consumers outside backend/alt_data/; the only IC results file (alt_data_ic_20260419T224855.tsv) is header-only; features.py:21 records 'FINRA: NOT attempted (adv_73 owner gate not cleared)'; the 13F row hardcodes ic=0.0 'outside this cycle's scope' (features.py:431-447); candidate_space.yaml:54 advertises the phantom mda_plus_alt_data_features bundle; finra_short.py's DAILY short_volume/total_volume (regsho daily TXT) is genuinely distinct from services/short_interest.py's bimonthly short-interest CSV (shrt{date}.csv, :37). So the infrastructure fully exists and the consumption layer + the actual IC run are the real gap -- 'finish the last mile' framing is accurate. The IC-first gate (IC_IR>0.5) bounds downside well and steps 1-2 are ~1 day at $0. Constraints: FINRA re-ingest sits behind the uncleared adv_73 owner gate (needs explicit operator sign-off, free data though); congress leg is Senate-only/sparse/stale-disclosure so expect weak IC (FINRA is the promising leg); the feature-vector wiring + ablation step again requires backtest runs currently barred by the historical_macro freeze, and final adoption is optimizer/gate-mediated, so P&L impact is deferred research-layer value. US-only data, no help for the 80 intl names.

vetted.title: Regime-conditioning via the orphaned VIX regime detector: regime feature column + per-regime rotation champions
vetted.notes: Orphan status verified: regime_detector.py's only consumers are spot_checks_harness.py:78-91 (result validation), tests, and settings; regime_detection_enabled defaults False (settings.py:153); feature vector has no vol-regime column (macro cols are fed_funds/cpi_yoy/unemployment/yield_curve/consumer_sentiment/treasury_10y); strategy_selector.py picks one champion on trailing DSR, regime-blind. BUT two big discounts: (1) regime-aware strategy selection was ALREADY TRIED -- commit ef5bca82 (Phase 1.6, turbulence-based regime->strategy mapping in label computation) was part of the Phase 1.2-1.9 bundle reverted in 9fbd9cd6 after a 27-window walk-forward showed the bundle degraded Sharpe; the proposal's step (c) is a cousin of that reverted idea layered onto a rotation stack the operator STOPPED as low money value (no deployment bridge to live selection -- rotation_runner.py docstring 'DEFERRED'); (2) live regime conditioning partially exists already via the macro_regime overlay (risk_on/off conviction multiplier + sector tilt, live since 51.1 revival). The genuinely new, cheap slice is step (a) vol_regime as a GBM feature column (+maybe (b) regime-matched sample weights) -- untried, additive, $0 VIX data. Hysteresis ban: not violated (proposal adds none; note strategy_selector.py itself already ships an approved 'anti-churn hysteresis', phase-47.6, so the ban clearly targeted paper-trading churn fixes). Same frozen-optimizer validation blocker as the other backtest-layer items; per-regime champions would also multiply strategy-switch churn, the exact cost class phase-61 flagged. 4-dev-day estimate only defensible for (a)+(b); (c) drags in the stopped rotation layer.

proposals.title: Live TCA capture: persist arrival price + implementation shortfall on every fill, retiring the synthetic-only TCA stub
proposals.description: Wire backend/services/tca.py::log_tca_event into paper_trader.execute_buy/execute_sell (arrival = price_at_analysis for buys, last mark/prior close for sells; fill = ExecutionRouter fill price), pass close_price through as reference_price in execution_router.submit_order's alpaca branch, and persist events to BigQuery (new arrival_price/is_bps columns on paper_trades via a scripts/migrations/ script, since _safe_save_trade prunes unknown columns) instead of the local handoff/tca_log.jsonl. Rewrite scripts/risk/tca_report.py to aggregate real rows (data_source='live') instead of unlinking the log and seeding synthetic fills. Append-only side channel: no decision logic is touched.
proposals.roi_rationale: TCA is a stub in the strongest sense: grep shows log_tca_event has ZERO production callers (only scripts/risk/tca_report.py), and that report deletes the log then seeds deterministic fake fills on every run (tca_report.py:120-123 'TCA_LOG_PATH.unlink()' + _seed_last_week) -- the system has never measured a real fill and would destroy real rows if they existed. execute_buy's trade dict (paper_trader.py:264-281) persists the fill price but discards price_at_analysis, so implementation shortfall can never be computed retroactively; execution_router.py:278 calls _alpaca_real_fill without the close_price reference. Phase-68 (pending) cuts over to REAL Alpaca fills in 68.3, and its design pack (design_execution_cutover_68.md) contains zero mentions of TCA/slippage/spread -- without this feature the first real-fill dataset lands unmeasured. The north-star promotion gate 'clears costs' currently has no realized-cost data source, and 61.5's static fee-table debate (FEE TABLE token) would instead run on measured per-market bps. In bq_sim mode IS is degenerate (fill==close), so risk now is nil and the payoff arrives exactly when 68.3 flips.

proposals.title: Rolling turnover budget at the allocator level (discretionary trades draw a NAV-normalized weekly budget; stops and kill-switch exempt)
proposals.description: Before decide_trades runs, compute trailing-N-session discretionary turnover (sum of |total_value| for trades whose reason is not stop_loss/kill_switch/take_profit, divided by NAV) from bigquery_client.get_paper_trades_in_window (:949, being repaired in 61.4), and pass the remaining budget into decide_trades. New BUYs and swap pairs that would exceed the budget are deferred with a logged, cycle-summary-visible reason; safety exits (stop_loss, kill_switch_auto_flatten, take_profit_2R/3R) are always exempt so the profit-making trailing-stop path is untouched. Config-gated default OFF with an ON-vs-OFF replay (replay_60_2_swap_fix.py pattern) and operator token to promote. This is a portfolio-level constraint (Garleanu-Pedersen 2013 is already in the operator's own 61.5 reference list), not a per-signal score band: paper_swap_min_delta_pct=25.0 is untouched, so it stays outside the banned hysteresis family.
proposals.roi_rationale: The live allocator has no turnover concept at all: portfolio_manager.decide_trades' only churn limiter is the count cap paper_swap_max_per_cycle=2 (settings.py:335), and the 2026-06-11 fleet audit showed the cap BECAME the cadence -- 5 of 6 trading days at exactly 2 BUYs + 2 SELLs, 81.4% weekly turnover, churn realized -$139.83 on <=2-day holds vs +$1,355.22 on long holds (goal_phase61_churn_integrity.md). Meanwhile perf_metrics.py:394-455 already defines compute_turnover_ratio + tx_cost_drag explicitly to stop the optimizer 'discovering churn alpha', but grep shows its only caller is backend/agents/skill_optimizer.py -- the penalty the backtest optimizer respects is invisible to the live engine, a measured inconsistency between what we optimize for and what we trade. 61.5's conditional min-holding lever is per-position and time-axis; a NAV-normalized budget bounds TOTAL discretionary churn regardless of which mechanism generates it (swap engine today, revived signal_downgrade tomorrow), closing the class rather than the instance. At the audited ~30%/yr N* drag pace, even halving discretionary churn is worth roughly $300+/yr on a $24K book -- recurring, not one-off.

proposals.title: Spread-aware fills and sizing: capture live NBBO bid/ask at order time, fill bq_sim at half-spread, downsize/skip when quoted cost exceeds expected edge
proposals.description: Extend the Alpaca snapshot call that execution_router._alpaca_real_fill already makes (execution_router.py:204-215) to read BOTH latestQuote.ap and .bp, persist the quoted spread on the fill (FillResult.raw + the TCA row from proposal 1), and add a flag-gated bq_sim mode that fills buys at close+half-spread / sells at close-half-spread for US names, falling back to 61.5's static per-market half-spread table for EU/KR (Alpaca has no KR/EU data; free-tier respected -- the paper account's data plan already serves this endpoint for the notional clamp). Second stage: a sizing hook in the buy loop that shrinks or skips a BUY when the quoted round-trip spread cost exceeds a configurable fraction of the position's expected edge. Also revive the dead ADV partial-fill model by passing adv through submit_order (today only shadow_submit accepts it). Default OFF with ON-vs-OFF 30-day replay, mirroring the 61.5 fee-table promotion mechanics.
proposals.roi_rationale: The default execution path is provably cost-blind: _bq_sim_fill (execution_router.py:83-125) fills at the exact close with zero spread -- audit finding 7 states KR paper P&L is overstated ~0.2% per round-trip, and all 3 KR entries in the audit window closed at a loss. The spread data is ALREADY being fetched and thrown away: execution_router.py:214 reads latestQuote.ap solely for the $10K notional clamp and never touches the bid. No slippage knob exists anywhere in settings.py (grep: zero hits), and the ADV partial-fill machinery (ADV_PARTIAL_FILL_THRESHOLD, :62,:103) is dead in production because paper_trader's submit_order call (paper_trader.py:256-259) never passes adv -- only harness parity scripts exercise it. 61.5 plans only a STATIC optional half-spread; measuring the live spread makes the paper book's costs honest BEFORE the 68.3 cutover exposes them for real, and prevents promoting strategies whose edge is thinner than their spread cost -- directly serving the 'clears costs' immutable gate.

proposals.title: Wire the wash-sale filter live with a BQ-reconstructed ledger (advisory mode first, blocking flag-gated)
proposals.description: At autonomous-cycle start, rebuild WashSaleLedger from one bounded BQ query over the last 61 days of SELL rows with realized_pnl_pct < 0 (the data already persists on every sell: paper_trades.realized_pnl_pct at paper_trader.py:441 and paper_round_trips.realized_pnl_usd at :460), then run candidate BUYs through the existing, tested filter_candidates before Step 7. Advisory mode (default): annotate the trade record and cycle summary with wash_sale=true + matched_sell_date + cumulative disallowed-loss dollars, so the operator sees the tax-adjusted cost of re-entry churn in real dollars. Blocking mode behind a default-OFF flag with operator token -- same sanctioned time-axis family as 61.5's conditional re-entry cooldown, but grounded in IRC 1091 tax reality rather than a churn heuristic.
proposals.roi_rationale: The module is doubly dead: grep shows filter_candidates has ZERO production callers (only scripts/compliance/wash_sale_filter.py --test and audit scripts), and WashSaleLedger.entries is a plain in-memory list with no persistence or reconstruction path -- even if someone called it, the ledger would start empty every cycle. Yet the audited churn pattern is exactly the wash-sale shape: SNDK/DELL round trips and 10 round-trips netting -$132 with re-entries days after loss exits. The module's own docstring states the learning-signal cost: 'a system that ignores wash sales over-reports realized losses and feeds the evaluator agent misleading P&L' -- so today's outcome_tracker reflections learn from loss numbers that would be partially disallowed in reality. With phase-68 driving toward real fills (and eventually real money), every disallowed loss silently raises the true after-tax cost of churn. This is the cheapest proposal here because the filter logic is already built and unit-tested; only the ledger reconstruction and the call site are new.

proposals.title: Same-cycle order netting + per-swap dollar-cost attribution (measurement only; no new blocking gate)
proposals.description: Two narrowly-scoped pieces. (a) A pure order-list transform between decide_trades and Step 7 execution that nets same-ticker SELL+BUY pairs into a single delta order -- stop-loss sells are exempt and never netted (the stop must stand); flag-gated default OFF. (b) Cost attribution: for every swap pair emitted by _compute_swap_candidates, compute and log the estimated round-trip dollar cost (fees from the 61.5 per-market table when ON, plus measured spread from proposals 1/3) into the cycle summary and paper_trades meta, so the operator's TURNOVER LEVERS decision at 61.5 runs on per-swap dollars instead of aggregate inference. Explicitly NOT included absent an operator token: any blocking benefit-vs-cost hurdle on the swap path, since paper_swap_min_delta_pct=25.0 is untouchable and an added gate could read as the banned band family.
proposals.roi_rationale: Two concrete gaps. First, decide_trades permits same-cycle full-exit-plus-re-entry: portfolio_manager.py:180 skips a candidate BUY only 'if ticker in held_tickers AND ticker not in selling_tickers' -- a ticker being sold this cycle falls through and can be re-bought, and autonomous_loop.py:1237-1295 executes all sells then all buys sequentially with no netting, paying two full fee+spread legs where the net delta might be a small trim (the MU pattern -- stop-sold then re-bought at a 7.6% higher price -- currently plays out cross-day, but nothing prevents the same-cycle variant, and netting is the standard Kitces-style rebalancing hygiene the operator's own goal doc references). Second, the swap engine trades roughly 2x position notional per swap (full SELL + full BUY, portfolio_manager.py:668-693) while evaluating only conviction delta and sector caps -- the dollar cost of a swap is computed NOWHERE in the codebase, so the system literally cannot report what its dominant discretionary trade type costs. Ranked last because the same-cycle netting frequency is not yet measured (the attribution piece produces exactly that evidence) and because anything near the swap path carries operator-ruling sensitivity.

vetted.title: Live TCA capture: persist arrival price + implementation shortfall on every fill, retiring the synthetic-only TCA stub
vetted.notes: Core claims VERIFIED in code: backend/services/tca.py::log_tca_event has zero production callers; scripts/risk/tca_report.py:120-123 unlinks the log and seeds synthetic fills every run (would destroy real rows); execute_buy receives price_at_analysis (paper_trader.py:135) but uses it only for the 30.6 tolerance gate and never persists it (trade dict :264-281); execution_router.py:277-278 drops close_price when calling _alpaca_real_fill; a scripts/migrations/ script is genuinely required (save_paper_trade inserts dynamic row keys; _safe_save_trade's schema-error retry prunes only _ROUND_TRIP_FIELDS, so an unmigrated arrival_price column would fail ALL trade saves -- implementation must handle this or trades break). HOWEVER the 'first real-fill dataset lands unmeasured' claim is overstated: masterplan 68.2 already requires 'every bq_sim fill paired with a shadow alpaca-paper order record in BQ... plus a drift report (fill-price and timing deltas)', 68.3 requires a <2% drift reconciliation report, and 68.6 a real-vs-sim divergence tracker. The genuine gap is the PERMANENT per-fill arrival-price/IS persistence and retiring the synthetic report. Also measured bps would be US-only (Alpaca has no EU/KR legs; bq_sim EU/KR IS measures only analysis-to-execution drift), so the 61.5 FEE TABLE debate stays static for KR/EU regardless. Best of the five, but should be folded INTO the phase-68 steps (68.2's paired-fill BQ persistence is the natural landing spot) rather than a parallel feature -- 68.3's success criteria require risk-path files byte-untouched during cutover, so an independent edit stream through paper_trader/execution_router mid-cutover adds coordination risk. No operator-policy conflict (append-only, $0, free APIs).

vetted.title: Rolling turnover budget at the allocator level (discretionary trades draw a NAV-normalized weekly budget; stops and kill-switch exempt)
vetted.notes: Facts verified: decide_trades has no turnover concept (only paper_swap_max_per_cycle=2, settings.py:335-340); perf_metrics compute_turnover_ratio/tx_cost_drag (:394-455) reach production only via skill_optimizer.get_scalar_metric_from_bq -- the optimizer-vs-live inconsistency is real. Not hysteresis-BANNED (the ban is 'score/rank-hysteresis family', goal_phase61_churn_integrity.md:139-141; a NAV budget is portfolio-level). BUT three killers: (1) the audited churn engine (sentinel-score swaps, 81.4% weekly turnover, -$139.83) was root-caused and its fix has been LIVE since 2026-06-12 -- 61.1 done, 'churn_fix True data_integrity True rj_binding True' (harness_log Cycle 66), replay suppressed 11/13 swaps -- so the headline ROI numbers describe a PRE-fix world; (2) pending 61.5 is the operator-designed measure-then-lever decision tree with an ENUMERATED sanctioned lever subset (min-holding / re-entry cooldown / score smoothing, each behind TURNOVER LEVERS tokens) and an explicit ELSE no-lever-needed branch -- a turnover budget is a new lever outside that subset, and building it now preempts the operator's decision and muddies the >=5-day churn measurement 61.5 requires; (3) the proposed data source bigquery_client.get_paper_trades_in_window (:949-964) is currently BROKEN (STRING created_at vs TIMESTAMP, BQ 400 x74, fix pending in 61.4). Sits at the decide_trades chokepoint of a working engine (medium regression risk, correctly self-assessed). Right move: offer the budget as an OPTION at the 61.5 operator checkpoint, not build it now.

vetted.title: Spread-aware fills and sizing: capture live NBBO bid/ask at order time, fill bq_sim at half-spread, downsize/skip when quoted cost exceeds expected edge
vetted.notes: Facts verified: _bq_sim_fill fills at exact close, zero spread (execution_router.py:83-125); no slippage/spread knob anywhere in settings.py (grep: zero hits); execution_router.py:214 reads latestTrade.p/latestQuote.ap only for the $10K clamp, bid discarded; ADV partial-fill dead in production (submit_order signature :271-273 has no adv; only shadow_submit, exercised solely by parity harness scripts). BUT: (1) the cited evidence is misattributed -- goal doc :85-86 says KR P&L is overstated ~0.2%/round-trip because of the MISSING KRX 0.20% sell-side TAX, which pending 61.5's static fee table fixes; live spread capture cannot help KR/EU at all (no Alpaca data, proposal concedes); (2) 61.5 already plans 'optional per-market half-spread bps' -- the static version of this proposal's bq_sim leg; (3) an NBBO abstraction ALREADY EXISTS unwired: backend/markets/broker_base.py::QuoteInfo + AlpacaBroker.get_quote (alpaca_broker.py:202-203 reads both bid and ask) -- extending the raw snapshot hack duplicates it; (4) the stage-2 sizing hook needs an expected-edge-in-bps quantity the system does not compute -- that is literally 61.5's score-to-forward-return slope criterion, so this depends on unbuilt pending work; (5) after 68.3 cutover, US fills are REAL Alpaca fills embodying actual spread -- the simulation-fidelity value window is the pre-cutover interim only; (6) US large-cap spreads on the names traded (AMD/MU class) are ~1-5 bps -- immaterial vs the audited churn drag ($17.14 total fees vs -$139.83 churn); (7) injecting a live data.alpaca.markets call into the default bq_sim path adds a network failure mode to the primary trading path of a working engine. The spread-persistence slice is subsumed by proposal 1 + 68.2 shadow fills.

vetted.title: Wire the wash-sale filter live with a BQ-reconstructed ledger (advisory mode first, blocking flag-gated)
vetted.notes: Factual claims all VERIFIED: backend/services/wash_sale_filter.py::filter_candidates has zero production callers (only scripts/compliance/wash_sale_filter.py --test + scripts/audit/regulatory_memo_audit.py); WashSaleLedger.entries is a plain in-memory list with no persistence; reconstruction data exists (paper_trades.realized_pnl_pct at paper_trader.py:441, paper_round_trips.realized_pnl_usd at :460); effort estimate is credible. But the PREMISE fails this operator and this system: (a) it is a PAPER account -- no tax events exist, so 'disallowed loss dollars' is a fiction column; (b) IRC Sec 1091 is US tax law and the operator is Norwegian (hotmail.no) -- Norwegian tax rules have no direct 1091 wash-sale equivalent, so even at real-money go-live the 61-day window models the wrong jurisdiction; (c) the 'misleading P&L for the evaluator' argument is economically wrong: a wash-sale disallowance DEFERS the loss into replacement-share basis for tax reporting -- the economic loss the outcome_tracker learns from is real and is exactly what the north-star (economic, risk/cost-adjusted P&L) optimizes; adjusting it would add noise; (d) blocking mode is functionally a 30-day post-loss re-entry cooldown -- the precise lever family 61.5 reserves to the operator's TURNOVER LEVERS token, so building a second cooldown mechanism duplicates sanctioned pending work under a tax costume. Cheap, yes; but the money value is ~zero and the one useful behavior (re-entry blocking) belongs to 61.5's operator decision.

vetted.title: Same-cycle order netting + per-swap dollar-cost attribution (measurement only; no new blocking gate)
vetted.notes: Part (a) targets a structurally impossible path: autonomous_loop.py:812 filters held tickers out of candidates BEFORE analysis ('new_candidates = [c for c in candidates if c["ticker"] not in held_tickers]'), so no BUY candidate can be a ticker sold in the same cycle -- the decide_trades:180 fall-through the proposal cites is unreachable for same-cycle re-buys in the production loop, and swap pairs are cross-ticker by construction (_compute_swap_candidates sells a holding, buys a sector-blocked candidate, portfolio_manager.py:668-693). The netting transform would net zero pairs; measured frequency is 0 by code, not by data. The real MU pattern is CROSS-day (stop-out removes the ticker from held_tickers so it re-enters candidates next cycle) -- netting cannot touch it, and the sanctioned instrument is 61.5's operator-gated re-entry cooldown. Part (b) is directionally real but small: the claim 'the dollar cost of a swap is computed NOWHERE' is only half-true -- each leg's transaction_cost is already persisted per paper_trades row (execute_buy :272, execute_sell :434), and swap legs are identifiable by reason strings (swap_for_higher_conviction / swap_buy), so per-swap dollars are a GROUP-BY away today; what is missing is pairing + spread, and 61.5's mandated ON-vs-OFF fee-table replay over the last 30 days of recorded trades produces the cost-restatement evidence the operator's TURNOVER LEVERS decision needs anyway. Correctly self-ranked last; the attribution logging alone is a ~half-day nicety, not a 2-day feature, and anything touching the swap path carries the operator-ruling sensitivity the proposal itself flags.

proposals.title: Intraday risk sentinel: enforce stops + kill-switch between daily cycles
proposals.description: Add a flag-gated (default OFF) APScheduler interval job (every 30-60 min, US regular trading hours first) that runs ONLY the existing defensive primitives: trader.mark_to_market() -> trader.check_stop_losses() -> execute_sell(reason='stop_loss_trigger') -> trader.check_and_enforce_kill_switch(). No screening, no analysis, no buys. Reuse cycle_lock.py so it never collides with the 10:00 ET full cycle, and reuse the idempotency already documented at autonomous_loop.py:1086-1090 ('execute_sell is naturally idempotent: get_position returns None if already sold'). Extend later to EU/KR sessions behind the same flag.
proposals.roi_rationale: The only trading cron is daily at 10:00 ET weekdays (backend/api/paper_trading.py:1302-1322 'Paper trading daily run'). Stop-loss enforcement (autonomous_loop.py:1100-1137), kill-switch breach evaluation (lines 1063-1084), and drawdown alarms (line 1551) ALL execute once inside that cycle -- so a position bought at 10:00 can blow through its stop by 20% intraday and nothing sells until 10:00 the next day, and the 4% daily-loss kill switch (kill_switch.py:230-264) mathematically cannot fire the day the loss happens. The book's proven protection (+20% NAV with trailing stops functioning, per the 2026-05-29 measurement) enforces at daily granularity only; this closes the largest always-on tail-loss window in the system using primitives that already exist and are idempotent. Loss cut at stop level instead of next-morning gap is direct net P&L.

proposals.title: Drawdown-conditional position-size scaler (fractional-Kelly ladder for new BUYs)
proposals.description: In portfolio_manager.decide_trades, after the Risk Judge pct is resolved (portfolio_manager.py:371-381), multiply position_pct by a deterministic monotone factor of current trailing drawdown, computed once per cycle from state the kill switch already tracks (KillSwitchState.snapshot() exposes peak_nav and sod_nav, kill_switch.py:126-149). Example ladder: 1.0 above -3% DD, 0.75 at -3..-5%, 0.5 at -5..-10% (aligned with the existing DRAWDOWN_TIERS in drawdown_alarm.py:36-40). Flag-gated default OFF, runtime-overridable via the risk_overrides pattern already used for caps (portfolio_manager.py:91-93, 303-310). Applies to NEW BUY sizing only -- never triggers sells or swaps, so it is explicitly not a hysteresis churn band (operator ban respected); computed from start-of-cycle NAV so it cannot flip-flop within a cycle.
proposals.roi_rationale: Risk response is currently binary: identical 10%-NAV default sizing at -1% and at -9.9% trailing DD, then a cliff to flatten+pause at -10% (kill_switch evaluate_breach; autonomous_loop.py:1063-1084 skips decide/execute entirely). The intermediate tiers exist ONLY as Slack alerts (drawdown_alarm.py:127 emit_drawdown_alarms fires pages, changes nothing). The project already imported the theory -- kelly_allocator.py cites MacLean/Thorp/Ziemba 2010 ('trades some asymptotic growth for dramatically reduced drawdowns') -- but fractional_kelly has ZERO production callers (grep: only scripts/audit/kelly_allocator_audit.py). Scaling exposure down as drawdown deepens is the cheapest known way to raise risk-adjusted P&L: it reduces variance drag and the chance of ever hitting the -10% flatten (which realizes losses at the worst point). All required data is already in memory; no new fetches.

proposals.title: Macro event-window risk gate: wire the econ calendar to BUY decisions
proposals.description: Add a flag-gated (default OFF) pre-BUY filter in decide_trades (or the candidate-build chokepoint at portfolio_manager.py:230-258, same placement as the binding RiskJudge gate) that queries pyfinagent_data.calendar_events for high-impact macro events (event_type in fomc_meeting/cpi/nfp) with scheduled_at inside the next N hours, and either skips new BUYs or halves position_pct on those days. Reuse the exclusion pattern already proven in sector_calendars.py (FDA binary-risk ±1-day filter, docstring lines 8-11). Bounded BQ read (date-filtered, LIMIT, 30s timeout per project rules) with fail-open on error.
proposals.roi_rationale: Evidence of an unexploited data column: blackout_start/blackout_end are computed (econ_calendar/blackout.py) and written to BQ (news/bq_writer.py:196-197) but grep shows ZERO readers anywhere in backend; fred_releases.py ingests CPI/NFP release dates explicitly 'for event-driven trading' and no trading code consumes them; the only calendar consumers are pead_signal.py:337 and sector_calendars.py:194, and sector_calendars points the WRONG direction for risk -- it BOOSTS scores +10% for earnings 1-3 days out. Concrete timing hole: the cycle buys at 10:00 ET (paper_trading.py:1306) while FOMC statements land 14:00 ET the same day (fed_scrape.py docstring), and stops are not enforced again until 10:00 next day -- a fresh FOMC-morning BUY carries the full announcement move with zero protection. Skipping or downsizing entries on ~16 scheduled binary-event days per quarter removes uncompensated event variance at near-zero opportunity cost (entry can wait one day).

proposals.title: Real Fama-French factor ingestion to un-stub the factor-crowding guard
proposals.description: Implement the deferred phase-40.8.2: fetch the Kenneth French Data Library daily FF3 CSV (free, no key) with a 24h local file cache (copy the proven _fetch_gpr_acts download+cache pattern in macro_regime.py:111-192), parse to {'Mkt-Rf','SMB','HML'} return series, and pass it as factor_returns to compute_candidate_loadings in autonomous_loop.py:445 (the parameter already exists, factor_loadings.py:57). Then run the operator's own documented rollout: quiet-log candidate-vs-portfolio cosine similarity for 1-2 weeks before enabling paper_max_factor_corr=0.85 (settings.py:285-293). Guard stays default OFF throughout.
proposals.roi_rationale: The factor-crowding guard is wired end-to-end (producer autonomous_loop.py:438-448, aggregator portfolio_manager.py:325-329, gate lines 410-424) but is mathematically vacuous: factor_loadings.py:31-37 synthetic_ff3_returns generates random.Random(4081).gauss noise, so compute_ff3 regresses real stock returns against RANDOM regressors -- betas are meaningless and the cosine-similarity cap would block or pass essentially at random if ever enabled. The module's own docstring admits 'Kenneth French ingestion deferred to phase-40.8.2' (factor_loadings.py:9) and grep confirms it never happened. The gap is live money-relevant: the operator memory notes only correlated strategies actually trade (triple_barrier/meta_label), and the AMD+MU buys of phase-66 are two names in one factor cluster the GICS sector cap treats as diversified. Real factor data converts an existing dead guard into cross-sector crowding protection, and simultaneously makes portfolio_risk.compute_ff3's beta cap meaningful for proposal 5.

proposals.title: Wire the orphaned CVaR tail-risk gate to real NAV history
proposals.description: Call portfolio_risk.daily_check() once per cycle before decide_trades, feeding it daily returns derived from the paper-portfolio snapshots the loop ALREADY fetches for the drawdown alarm (bq.get_paper_snapshots(limit=180), autonomous_loop.py:1557-1560; sort ASC by snapshot_date per the phase-66.2 DESC-trap lesson in drawdown_alarm.py:66-100). Flag-gated default OFF: when gate.new_positions_allowed is false (CVaR_97.5 > 2%), skip new BUYs for the cycle and log blocking_reasons; sells/stops always still run. Fail-open on any error, and require data_source=='live' (never act on the seeded fixture). Optionally surface the same result through the stub MCP tools so the MAS layer stops receiving placeholders.
proposals.roi_rationale: A complete, tested tail-risk gate exists and is dead code: portfolio_risk.daily_check (CVAR_LIMIT_PCT=0.02, BETA_CAP=1.5, returns gate.new_positions_allowed) has ZERO callers -- the only import of the module anywhere is factor_loadings.py pulling compute_ff3. The MCP surface confirms the wiring never landed: risk_server.py portfolio_cvar and factor_exposure are self-declared 'Stub placeholder. Real implementation lives in phase-4.8 step 4.8.2'. Meanwhile the real input data (180 daily NAV snapshots) is already fetched every cycle three lines from where the gate should sit. A realized-CVaR circuit breaker is the portfolio-level complement to per-position stops: it stops ADDING exposure when recent NAV returns show fat left tails (which is exactly when position-level correlations have spiked and diversification has failed), protecting risk-adjusted P&L without touching existing holdings. Near-pure wiring work around an immutable-gate-compatible design (adds a guard; relaxes nothing).

vetted.title: Intraday risk sentinel: enforce stops + kill-switch between daily cycles
vetted.notes: GAP VERIFIED. Only trading cron is daily 10:00 ET mon-fri (backend/api/paper_trading.py:1299-1322); stop-loss + kill-switch run once per cycle (autonomous_loop.py:1063-1137); no intraday job exists (all add_job sites checked; slack-bot watchdog is health-only). Stronger than claimed: the 4% daily-loss kill switch is a DEAD SWITCH on the scheduled cadence -- check_and_enforce_kill_switch (paper_trader.py:1043-1058) re-anchors SOD NAV in the same call that evaluates breach, so daily_loss_pct is ~0 at every scheduled evaluation; only trailing-DD can ever fire, next morning. cycle_lock.py exists (fcntl flock, reusable) and the execute_sell idempotency comment is verbatim at autonomous_loop.py:1086-1090. NOT banned (local APScheduler, free yfinance, not hysteresis). CAUTION that caps the score: mark_to_market internally calls _advance_stop (paper_trader.py:553) and rewrites MFE/MAE + every position row in BQ -- intraday cadence makes the trailing-stop engine ratchet on intraday peaks (tighter effective stops, more whipsaw exits), which brushes the operator ruling 'trailing-stop engine untouchable' by behavior; a correct implementation should price-check stops WITHOUT the advance side effect, i.e. not reuse mark_to_market as-is. _get_live_price is an uncached per-ticker yfinance call (rate/latency fine for paper, ~13 extra runs/day). Largest genuine tail-risk hole of the five; flag-gated OFF design is right; medium regression risk is honestly stated.

vetted.title: Drawdown-conditional position-size scaler (fractional-Kelly ladder for new BUYs)
vetted.notes: GAP VERIFIED. No deterministic DD-conditional sizing exists: sizing is Risk-Judge pct or flat 10% default (portfolio_manager.py:371-381); zero drawdown references in portfolio_manager; recent_drawdown_pct feeds ONLY the advisory alpha-decay LLM agent (orchestrator.py:1310-1341), and the Risk Judge prompt gets static limits, not live DD. fractional_kelly has zero production callers (only scripts/audit/kelly_allocator_audit.py) -- claim exact. kill_switch snapshot exposes peak_nav/sod_nav in-memory (kill_switch.py:137-145); DRAWDOWN_TIERS alert-only (drawdown_alarm.py:36-40, 127); risk_overrides get_effective pattern confirmed (portfolio_manager.py:91-93, 303-310). 'No new fetches' claim TRUE. POLICY: not banned by the letter -- 53.1/55.3 ban targets score-band/no-trade-band swap-churn designs; this scales BUY size only, causes no sells/round-trips -- but 55.3's 'same-family in any renamed form' precedent is expansive and tier boundaries can flip the factor across cycles; requires proactive family disclosure to Q/A + operator sign-off (the 61.5 research brief models exactly this disclosure for score-smoothing). Cheap (1.5-2 days), low regression (BUY sizing only, flag OFF). Score capped at 5 because it is insurance value in drawdown regimes the +20% paper book has not yet entered, not direct P&L generation, and one operator-authorization round-trip is likely.

vetted.title: Macro event-window risk gate: wire the econ calendar to BUY decisions
vetted.notes: GATE GAP REAL, DATA FOUNDATION VAPOR. No macro-event awareness exists in any BUY path (verified), the FOMC timing hole is real (buy 10:00 ET cron, FOMC statement 14:00 ET per fed_scrape.py docstring, next stop check 10:00 next day), and blackout_start/blackout_end are written with zero readers (news/bq_writer.py:196-197). BUT: read-only BQ query confirms pyfinagent_data.calendar_events is EMPTY (0 rows), and the ENTIRE ingestion pipeline is orphaned end-to-end -- econ_calendar.watcher.run_once and write_calendar_events have ZERO production callers (no cron in main.py, slack_bot/jobs/, or scripts; table only created 2026-06-11 after the reserved-keyword bug). The existing 'consumers' (pead_signal.py:337, sector_calendars.py:194) query the empty table and fail-open to [] -- the sector_calendars earnings-boost 'wrong direction' argument is about dead code. True effort = ingestion cron + FRED_API_KEY config + fed_scrape/fred_releases verification + backfill BEFORE the stated 2-day gate work (~2x estimate). P&L sign is also ambiguous: pre-FOMC announcement drift (Lucca-Moench) is famously POSITIVE, so blanket-skipping event-day entries may cost more expected return than the variance it saves; the case is risk-cosmetic rather than net-P&L. Not operator-banned (free FRED API, bounded fail-open reads, no historical_macro contact).

vetted.title: Real Fama-French factor ingestion to un-stub the factor-crowding guard
vetted.notes: GATE SKELETON EXISTS DARK; PROPOSAL FIXES 1 OF 3 DEAD INPUTS. Verified: synthetic random.Random(4081) factors (factor_loadings.py:31-37), docstring admits phase-40.8.2 deferral (line 9), producer wired at autonomous_loop.py:438-448 behind enable_factor_loadings=OFF, gate at portfolio_manager.py:410-424 behind paper_max_factor_corr=0.0 (settings.py:288-293), Kenneth French ingestion truly absent, macro_regime._fetch_gpr_acts download+cache pattern exists to copy. MISSED BY THE PROPOSAL: (a) the screener NEVER attaches price_history to candidate rows (screener.py:218-229 row dict has no such key), so compute_candidate_loadings receives empty windows and sets every candidate loading to None even with real factor data; (b) positions never persist factor_loadings (execute_buy puts it on the TRADE dict only, paper_trader.py:287-288; zero factor migrations in scripts/migrations/), so aggregate_portfolio_loadings returns {} and the gate short-circuits at portfolio_manager.py:413 regardless. Real scope = French CSV ingestion + screener price plumbing + position persistence/migration + 1-2wk quiet-log: roughly 2x the 2-3-day estimate. Money case weak: the AMD+MU example is factually wrong -- both are GICS Information Technology, already one cluster under the 2-per-sector count cap; residual cross-sector crowding on a ~5-15 name, ~66%-cash book with count + 30%-NAV sector caps is thin. Not banned (free data, local cache, no optimizer/historical_macro contact).

vetted.title: Wire the orphaned CVaR tail-risk gate to real NAV history
vetted.notes: ORPHAN CONFIRMED, WIRING CHEAP, PROTECTION MARGINAL. portfolio_risk.daily_check (CVAR_LIMIT_PCT=0.02, BETA_CAP=1.5, gate.new_positions_allowed) is complete, tested, and has zero production callers -- only scripts/audit/portfolio_risk_audit.py exercises it and the only backend import of the module is factor_loadings pulling compute_ff3. MCP risk_server portfolio_cvar/factor_exposure are self-declared 'Stub placeholder' (verified in tool descriptions). Snapshot fetch exists at autonomous_loop.py:1555-1560 but in the FINALLY block after the cycle -- pre-decide placement needs a hoist or second get_paper_snapshots call (trivial; DESC-sort trap already documented in drawdown_alarm.py:66-100). Implementation subtleties that dilute 'near-pure wiring': (1) NAV-diff returns are deposit-contaminated -- the deposit endpoint (paper_trading.py:1200-1272) changes NAV without being a return and deposits DID occur (the $25 window), so returns must be flow-adjusted or CVaR is corrupted; (2) the proposed data_source=='live' guard can never pass as written -- passing only portfolio_returns yields 'partial_seeded' because factor_returns stay seeded, and the BETA_CAP leg is meaningless until real FF3 lands (proposal 4 dependency); use the CVaR leg only. Not banned; adds a guard, relaxes nothing; fail-open + flag OFF. Score 4: genuinely 1-2 days and low regression risk, but a realized-CVaR buy-blocker overlaps heavily with kill-switch trailing-DD tiers, per-position stops, and sector caps on a 66%-cash book -- it rarely binds, so expected P&L delta is small.

proposals.title: Trade post-mortem engine: Fable-authored root-cause reviews of every closed losing trade, feeding the dead prompt_patch_queue and agent_memories
proposals.description: A weekly DEV-TIME job on the Claude Code Max rail (Fable during the free window, Opus after): read closed round-trips from paper_trades (realized_pnl_pct, holding_days, commissions, stop_loss_trigger vs. swap vs. rec-change exits) joined with the full decision_traces already persisted in analysis_results.full_report_json, and author structured post-mortems with a fixed taxonomy (bad entry thesis / bad exit / churn swap that never cleared round-trip cost / sizing error / data gap). Each post-mortem produces two cheap runtime artifacts: (a) high-quality lesson rows written via the existing bq.save_agent_memory -> BM25 {{past_memory}} injection, and (b) targeted prompt patches enqueued into the already-built intel_prompt_patches approve/reject state machine, applied to skills/*.md modifiable sections only after operator approval (mtime cache auto-picks up edits, zero code change). Extends reflection coverage beyond the current 4 agents and finally includes cost/churn context, which phase-61 identified as the real P&L sink. Compliant with the hysteresis ban: outputs are prompts and memories, not switching bands.
proposals.roi_rationale: Direct answer to 'is prompt_patch_queue fed?': NO. backend/intel/prompt_patch_queue.py has zero production feeders or consumers (grep hits only tests, scripts/smoketest/intel_e2e.py, and the DDL migration); no API or service imports backend.intel. Meanwhile the only learning loop that exists is a 2-3 sentence Gemini reflection (memory.py:213-254) that sees NO fees, NO exit reason, NO debate content, covers only 4 of 28 agents (outcome_tracker.py:25 REFLECTION_AGENTS), and is gated behind paper_learn_loop_enabled which defaults False (settings.py:33). The phase-47.7 comment in autonomous_loop.py:2722-2730 shows the loop silently recorded 0.0 return for every sell-close for months. Phase-61's 14/14 fleet audit pinned churn (not fees) as the real cost, yet no artifact anywhere teaches the agents about it. Expensive reasoning happens at dev time; runtime cost is $0 (BQ rows + markdown).

proposals.title: Golden replay corpus + Fable-authored upgrades of the 28 Gemini skill prompts (replace the never-run runtime SkillOptimizer loop)
proposals.description: Build a one-time frozen evaluation corpus at dev time from data that already exists: analysis_results.full_report_json carries per-agent decision_traces (orchestrator.py:2189 attaches every agent's signal/confidence/evidence/reasoning) and outcome_tracking carries realized returns. Run skill_optimizer.analyze_agent_performance's per-agent signal-vs-outcome scoring (already coded, never executed) over the full corpus to rank the weakest agents. Then, on the Max rail, Fable rewrites the 2-3 weakest skills/*.md prompts with the entire evidence corpus in context (which agents' BULLISH calls preceded losses, which anti-patterns fired, etc.), validates candidate prompts by re-judging held-out corpus rows as an LLM judge at dev time, and ships winners as git-diffed .md files with entries appended to skill_results.tsv. Runtime is unchanged: Gemini reads the same skill files via the mtime cache. One agent per cycle, operator reviews the diff, instant git revert.
proposals.roi_rationale: The 28 skill prompts ARE the daily money engine (they produce the BUY/SELL/sizing decisions), and the system's own improvement loop for them has never fired once: backend/agents/skills/experiments/skill_results.tsv is 76 bytes (header only, created Apr 24), and every skill's Experiment Log table shows only the baseline row. Root cause is visible in the design: compute_metric() is a noisy whole-portfolio scalar needing weeks of live trading per experiment, and proposals come from runtime Gemini at temperature 0.7 with only 6000 chars of context (skill_optimizer.py:325-372). Offline replay evaluation removes the weeks-long feedback bottleneck and substitutes frontier reasoning for Flash-tier proposal generation, at $0 metered cost.

proposals.title: Evidence-anchored confidence calibration: replace hand-invented confidence bands and Risk Judge sizing tiers with measured base rates
proposals.description: Dev-time Fable study on the Max rail: pull every (recommendation_confidence, risk_adjusted_confidence, recommended_position_pct, realized return_pct, holding_days) tuple from analysis_results + outcome_tracking + paper_trades, compute realized hit-rates and return distributions per confidence band and per Risk Judge decision tier, and author two artifacts: (1) a rewritten calibration section for the shared house system prompt in llm_client.py and risk_judge.md that anchors each band to its MEASURED base rate ('confidence 70-89 recommendations realized +X% median over 30d, N=..., so treat 70-89 as...'), and (2) an optional flag-gated deterministic clamp table in config mapping decision tier -> max position_pct, default OFF. Ship the prompt-context artifact first (advisory), the clamp only after operator review.
proposals.roi_rationale: Position sizing is where confidence becomes money, and every number in that path is currently invented rather than measured: the house-prompt calibration table (llm_client.py _HOUSE_INSTRUCTIONS, 'Recommendation calibration' mandate 3) and risk_judge.md's APPROVE_FULL=5-10% / APPROVE_REDUCED=2-5% bands were hand-written, and portfolio_manager.py:200-207+377-380 consumes the LLM's recommended_position_pct directly (10% fallback) into target_amount = nav * position_pct/100. A repo-wide grep for calibration validation finds none — the data to do it (88-column analysis_results, outcome_tracking rows written on sell-closes since phase-47.7) has never been used. Mis-calibrated sizing is a pure risk-adjusted-P&L leak on an engine that is otherwise working; fixing it needs deep statistical reasoning once, then zero runtime cost.

proposals.title: Market-aware prompt annexes for the live EU/KR books (KR disclosure regime, EU microstructure) injected only for non-US symbols
proposals.description: Fable authors, at dev time, compact per-market analysis annexes: Korea (DART filings instead of EDGAR, chaebol cross-holdings, KRW reporting, retail-heavy flow, price-limit bands), Germany/EU (ad-hoc disclosure regime, IFRS vs GAAP framing, XETRA auction microstructure), plus explicit 'this US-only data source is absent for this market — treat as no-signal, not negative signal' guidance for insider/options/patent agents whose feeds are US-centric. Delivered as a market-conditional section injected into the FACT_LEDGER/context assembly only when backend.backtest.markets.market_for_symbol(ticker) != 'US' (one small, well-tested code seam), leaving the proven US path byte-identical. Includes a dev-time Fable audit of a sample of recent KR/EU analysis_results decision_traces to catch US-assumption hallucinations already occurring.
proposals.roi_rationale: Real capital exposure exists NOW with untested prompt quality: EU+KR paper trading went live 2026-06-01, and orchestrator.py:350-357 + 468-473 confirms non-SEC symbols (005930.KS, SAP.DE) run the same 26+ agents — but the shared house prompt is US-anchored throughout (Pillar 5 cites 'Material 8-K filings', the safety anchor cites the SEC 1940 Act, cap tiers are USD, the sector table lists only US tickers), and a grep of all 28 skills finds zero KR/EU content. Agents reasoning about Korean tickers with SEC-shaped priors mis-read disclosure absence as negative signal and mis-frame valuation context — a systematic quality drag on exactly the newest books. Deep market-structure reasoning happens once at dev time; the artifact is static text.

proposals.title: Resurrect the dead directive-evolution loop as a dev-time Fable workflow (rewriter + fail-closed reviewer are fully built, never called)
proposals.description: backend/meta_evolution/directive_rewriter.py (SIPDO-pattern rewriter with anti-drift floors, global-confirmation gate, BQ lineage via directive_versions) and directive_review.py (independent 5-dimension fail-closed judge) are complete, tested modules with ZERO production callers — run_meta_evolution_cycle (cron.py:87-179) never invokes them. Rather than wiring their runtime LLM calls (which would hit the METERED anthropic sk-ant-api path in _call_llm_for_rewrite — a $0-policy violation), add a masterplan-protocol step where the Layer-3 harness itself executes the loop on the Max rail: parse recent research-brief JSON envelopes from handoff/archive/, apply _summarize_brief_signals + should_apply_globally as the deterministic checklist, have Fable propose the directive diff, run review_directive_diff's rubric as the Q/A evaluation frame, persist DirectiveVersion rows via the existing persist_version, and keep the existing HITL apply gate (operator approves, session restart).
proposals.roi_rationale: Indirect but compounding ROI with near-zero risk: every money-focused dev cycle starts with the research gate, and the empirical signals to improve it (gate_pass rates, sources-in-full, downstream Q/A verdicts) are collected but never acted on — the machinery to act on them was built in phases 10.7.2/10.7.7 and then orphaned (grep: no callers of rewrite_directive/review_directive_diff/should_apply_globally outside tests and the defining modules). This is sunk-cost recovery: ~1 day of wiring turns two dead, reviewed modules into a working self-improvement loop on the flat-fee rail, and its anti-drift floors (MIN_BRIEFS=5, judge floor 0.6, fail-closed review, HITL apply) are already the safety design a reviewer would demand.

vetted.title: Trade post-mortem engine: Fable-authored root-cause reviews of every closed losing trade, feeding the dead prompt_patch_queue and agent_memories
vetted.notes: Dead-feeder claim VERIFIED: backend/intel/prompt_patch_queue.py has zero production callers (grep: only tests, scripts/smoketest/intel_e2e.py, scripts/migrations/phase_6_5_intel_schema.py; the whole backend.intel package is orphaned) — and note there is also NO applier that writes approved patches into skills/*.md and NO API/UI for approve/reject, so more is missing than claimed. Thin-reflection claims VERIFIED: memory.py:213-254 is a 2-3 sentence Gemini lesson with no fees/exit_reason (though the situation string DOES include a one-line debate consensus via build_situation_description — 'sees NO debate content' is overstated); REFLECTION_AGENTS=4 at outcome_tracker.py:25; {{past_memory}} injection only reaches ~7 debate/risk agents (debate.py:225-310, risk_debate.py:202-281), not 28, so lesson leverage is narrower than pitched. paper_learn_loop_enabled defaults False (settings.py:33) and was NEVER flipped — outcome_tracking + agent_memories have 0 rows ever (phase-68 goal doc, audit basis #3). CRITICAL OVERLAP: active masterplan step 68.4 already owns learn-loop activation (DARK until operator token PAPER-LEARN-LOOP: ENABLE) — this proposal is a rich layer on top of a step the operator has already sequenced. Churn premise is HALF-DEAD: phase-61 pinned churn to a code defect (sentinel denominator, portfolio_manager.py:561, fix shipped dark in commit 7f0de140 behind paper_swap_churn_fix_enabled; replay suppresses 11/13 swaps) — prompt lessons about churn would not have prevented it. paper_trades has exit_reason/realized_pnl_pct/holding_days/MFE/MAE (paper_trader.py:409-466) but NO commissions field (flat 0.1%/side lives at execution level). Not banned (outputs are prompts/memories, not switching bands). Sequencing kills near-term ROI: the entire fill history is synthetic (phase-68 goal: 'every fill ever made is synthetic'), real fills arrive via 68.2/68.3, alpha work is deferred to phase-69, and new agent_memories rows change runtime prompt content during the divergence-measurement window. Build AFTER 68.4's token + a real-fill window.

vetted.title: Golden replay corpus + Fable-authored upgrades of the 28 Gemini skill prompts (replace the never-run runtime SkillOptimizer loop)
vetted.notes: Never-ran claim VERIFIED: skill_results.tsv is 76 bytes header-only (created Apr 24); the full runtime machinery exists unpaid — SkillOptimizer + API endpoint POST /api/skills/optimize (backend/api/skills.py:18-60) + MetaCoordinator skill_opt action — it just never fired. Design-flaw claims VERIFIED: compute_metric is the whole-portfolio scalar via perf_metrics.get_scalar_metric_from_bq (skill_optimizer.py:115-125); proposal generation uses temperature 0.7 with current_skill[:6000] (skill_optimizer.py:333, 370); decision_traces attach to full_report_json (orchestrator.py:~2189). BUT a load-bearing data premise is FALSE: analyze_agent_performance (skill_optimizer.py:152-266) joins outcome_tracking, which has 0 rows EVER (phase-68 goal audit basis #3 — learn loop never enabled) — running the 'already coded, never executed' scorer today returns all-0.5 neutral scores. The corpus's outcome labels must be NEWLY constructed from paper_trades realized_pnl_pct or price history, and the joinable sample is further shrunk because 9/10 churn-swap-displaced positions were UNANALYZED (no same-cycle analysis_results row, phase-61 goal doc). Per-agent credit assignment from traces to portfolio P&L remains the same hard problem the runtime loop had; an LLM judge on held-out rows is a weak validator. Not banned, but TIMING CONFLICTS with the active operator goal: phase-68 explicitly holds strategy constant while measuring shadow/cutover divergence ('Alpha re-opening DEFERRED to phase-69') — rewriting the 28 decision prompts IS a strategy change and would contaminate 68.2/68.6 measurements. Medium regression risk on the literal money path. Defensible as a phase-69 candidate, not now.

vetted.title: Evidence-anchored confidence calibration: replace hand-invented confidence bands and Risk Judge sizing tiers with measured base rates
vetted.notes: Gap is REAL and all code claims VERIFIED: hand-written calibration mandate at llm_client.py:75 (_HOUSE_INSTRUCTIONS mandate 3); hand-written APPROVE_FULL=5-10%/APPROVE_REDUCED=2-5% tiers at risk_judge.md:43; portfolio_manager.py:377-381 does target_amount = nav*(position_pct/100) with a 10% default when the judge omits pct (plus phase-66.2 RJ-shape flag handling at :196-209); repo-wide grep finds ZERO calibration-validation code (only prompt text and unrelated cache hit-rates). The confidence/sizing columns ARE persisted (bigquery_client.py:183/211/240: recommendation_confidence, risk_adjusted_confidence, recommended_position_pct). BUT the stated outcome source is FALSE: outcome_tracking has 0 rows ever (flag never flipped; phase-68 goal audit basis #3) — 'rows written on sell-closes since phase-47.7' never happened. The study must join analysis_results with paper_trades realized_pnl_pct instead (feasible; exit_reason exists at paper_trader.py:466). Bigger discount: the realized-return history is contaminated — 11/16 audited sells were <=2-day sentinel-churn artifacts (phase-61 goal doc) on synthetic fills, so 'measured base rates' from this book risk anchoring prompts to bug-driven exits, and per-band sample sizes will be thin. The advisory-prompt-first + flag-gated-clamp-default-OFF design matches house dark-flag discipline and is not hysteresis (no switching bands). Best sequencing: run the study after 68.3 real Alpaca-paper fills accumulate a clean window; as pitched today it 'measures' a corrupted distribution. Direct sizing-to-money link keeps this at 4 despite the data caveats.

vetted.title: Market-aware prompt annexes for the live EU/KR books (KR disclosure regime, EU microstructure) injected only for non-US symbols
vetted.notes: Gap VERIFIED at the prompt layer: grep across all 29 skills/*.md finds zero DART/KOSPI/XETRA/IFRS/chaebol/KRW content; house prompt is US-anchored exactly as claimed (Material 8-K at llm_client.py:123, SEC 1940 Act concentration threshold at :230). Non-US symbols DO run the same 26+ agents (phase-60.1: orchestrator.py:349-380 _quant_from_yfinance, :466-473 _is_sec_covered, :1634-1692 explicit skipped_stages), and the claimed injection seam exists cleanly (markets.py:142 market_for_symbol; single FACT_LEDGER assembly at orchestrator.py:386 + :1731 feeding every agent prompt). PARTIAL MITIGATION the proposal undersells: the phase-60.1 data layer already labels absence explicitly ('SEC EDGAR skipped (non-US listing)', 'RAG corpus is SEC 10-K/10-Q only') and persists skipped_stages into full_report_json — so US-only data absence is visibly reasoned, just not weighted by prompt guidance; the 'mis-read absence as negative signal' harm is plausible but unmeasured speculation. Not banned; static dev-time text + one conditional seam keeps the US path byte-identical = genuinely low regression risk, and the cheapest effort of the five. ROI capped by context: EU/KR book share is small (multi-market live 06-01, engine stalled early July, current reactivation buys are US: AMD/MU), phase-68's entire focus is the US Alpaca real-fill runway (the 115-line cutover design doc never mentions KR/EU), and the go-live evidence chain runs on US fills. Reasonable phase-69-adjacent hygiene; the dev-time audit of existing KR/EU decision_traces for US-assumption hallucinations is the highest-value slice and nearly free.

vetted.title: Resurrect the dead directive-evolution loop as a dev-time Fable workflow (rewriter + fail-closed reviewer are fully built, never called)
vetted.notes: Dead-code claims FULLY VERIFIED: rewrite_directive/review_directive_diff/should_apply_globally/persist_version have zero callers outside tests and each other (agent_map.py:66-67 is just a name-to-model label); run_meta_evolution_cycle (cron.py:87-179, read in full) invokes only cron_allocator, provider_rebalancer, and archetype_library — the directive modules were orphaned at birth. Metered-API claim VERIFIED: _call_llm_for_rewrite's primary path is the raw Anthropic SDK with sk-ant-api key on claude-sonnet-4-6 (directive_rewriter.py:172-186), so runtime wiring would violate the $0-metered policy; the dev-time Layer-3 reframe correctly avoids that, and the anti-drift floors are real (MIN_BRIEFS_FOR_PROPOSAL=5, MIN_LLM_JUDGE_SCORE=0.6 at directive_rewriter.py:41 + docstring; fail-closed review confirmed in directive_review.py). BUT the FUNCTION is not actually missing: researcher.md is already actively evolved through the standard harness process with operator review — phase-67.3 codified write-first, phase-29.7 added the PDF-fetch chain, the research-gate rules doc is continuously amended — i.e., the harness itself IS the directive-improvement loop, with richer context than the rewriter's shallow scalar signals (gate_pass_rate, avg_sources at directive_rewriter.py:100-122), which are process-compliance metrics, not money metrics. Marginal value over status quo ~epsilon P&L; 'sunk-cost recovery' is not ROI. Also underweighted friction: .claude/agents edits require session-restart snapshot verification plus the separation-of-duties rule (Peder review before dependent steps). Not banned, genuinely low risk, ~1 day — but it optimizes the meta-meta layer while the operator's active goal is real fills. Skip unless a measured research-gate failure pattern emerges.
